title: Limiting Argument (§2): Backward inequality direction in reduction to simple roots

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
## Issue

Lines 148–165 claim:

"LHS: 1/Φ_n(p ⊞_n q) ≥ limsup_{ε→0} 1/Φ_n(T_ε²(p ⊞_n q))"

But this direction is problematic given lower semicontinuity of Φ_n.

## Analysis

If Φ_n is lower semicontinuous (LSC) and T_ε²(p ⊞_n q) → p ⊞_n q as ε→0, then:

liminf_{ε→0} Φ_n(T_ε²(p ⊞_n q)) ≥ Φ_n(p ⊞_n q)

Taking reciprocals (with 1/x decreasing):

limsup_{ε→0} 1/Φ_n(T_ε²(p ⊞_n q)) ≤ 1/Φ_n(p ⊞_n q)

This is the OPPOSITE direction from what is claimed.

## What's Missing

1. **Convergence verification**: Does T_ε²(p ⊞_n q) → p ⊞_n q as ε→0? This requires showing:
   - T_ε p → p and T_ε q → q (as polynomials)
   - By continuity of root map: roots converge
   - By continuity of ⊞_n on roots: T_ε²(p ⊞_n q) → p ⊞_n q

2. **Limiting argument**: If convergence holds, take limits of both sides:
   1/Φ_n(T_ε²(p ⊞_n q)) ≥ 1/Φ_n(T_ε p) + 1/Φ_n(T_ε q)
   ↓
   1/Φ_n(p ⊞_n q) ≥ 1/Φ_n(p) + 1/Φ_n(q)
   
   This works by continuity, but uses limsup/liminf incorrectly.

## Impact

The reduction to simple roots is essential. Without a correct limiting argument, the extension from simple-root case to general roots is not justified.
